recipes.title: Bourbon Carrot Cake with Cream Cheese Frosting
recipes.ingredients:
2 cups all-purpose flour spooned & leveled
2 teaspoons baking powder
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 1⁄2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
1⁄2 teaspoon ground ginger
1⁄4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
1⁄2 teaspoon salt
3⁄4 cup canola or vegetable oil
4 large eggs at room temperature
1 1⁄2 cups packed light brown sugar
1⁄2 cup granulated sugar
1⁄2 cup unsweetened applesauce
1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
3 tablespoons Bourbon
3 cups lightly packed grated carrots (do not grate fine - use larger grater)
¾ cups raisins
1 cups coarsely chopped almonds or pecans or walnuts (optional)
For the cream cheese frosting:
1 (8-ounce) package brick style cream cheese softened
1⁄2 cup unsalted butter softened
2+ cups powdered sugar
1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
2 tablespoons Bourbon
recipes.directions:
To make the carrot cake: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spray two 9-inch round cake pans well with non-stick cooking spray and line the bottom of each pan with a parchment paper circle. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and salt until well combined. Set aside.
In a separate large mixing bowl, whisk together the oil, eggs, brown sugar, granulated sugar, applesauce, and vanilla extract until fully combined. Add the grated carrots to the wet ingredients and mix until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a whisk or rubber spatula until just combined, making sure not to over mix the batter.
Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes 2 9-inch round cake pans for 35 minutes.  To make a 9x13 cake: Spray a 9x13 pan with nonstick cooking spray and pour the batter into the pan. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes.
recipes.notes:
Freezing Instructions: Cake and frosting will freeze well for up to 3 months, thaw overnight in the fridge and bring to
room temperature before assembling the cake.
Applesauce: You may replace the applesauce with 1/2 cup of sour cream or plain Greek yogurt.

recipes.title: Spice Cake
recipes.ingredients:
2 3/4 cups (350g) all purpose flour
1 1/2 tsp baking powder
1 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
1 tbsp cinnamon
1 tsp cardamom
1 tsp ginger
1/2 tsp nutmeg
1/4 tsp cloves
2 cups (380g) light brown sugar, packed
1 cup (200ml) vegetable or canola oil
1 cup (235g) unsweetened apple sauce
4 large eggs, room temperature
3 tsp vanilla extract
3/4 cup (200g) sour cream, room temperature
recipes.directions:
Preheat the oven to 350F and prep (2) 8″ round cake pans. Coat them with a thin layer of nonstick spray and line the bottoms with a round sheet of parchment paper. Wrap in damp cake strips if you have them. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and spices. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, either mixing by hand with a whisk or an electric mixer, mix together the sugar, oil, and apple sauce until the sugar is evenly moistened.
Whisk in the eggs, one at a time, followed by the vanilla.
Once smooth, mix in half of the dry ingredients until combined. Then mix in the sour cream, followed by the rest of the dry ingredients.
The batter should be fairly thin. Pour it into the two cake pans, filling each about 2/3 full.
Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick in the center comes out clean. While the cake bakes, make the brown butter.
recipes.notes: Goes well with Penuche Frosting

recipes.title: Elevated Box Cake
recipes.ingredients:
See cake box
Milk instead of water
Melted butter instead of oil (if it calls for ⅓ oil do ½ butter)
Add an extra egg
Add pudding mix packet in same or complimenting flavor
Add vanilla extract
Chocolate cake specific
Add cocoa powder
Coffee instead of water
If milk instead of water instant espresso powder
recipes.directions: Follow the directions on the box

recipes.title: Soft Lemon Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
3 cups (360 g) all-purpose flour, spooned and leveled
1 teaspoon baking powder
½ teaspoon baking soda
½ teaspoon salt
1 cup (2 sticks, 226 g) unsalted butter, softened
1 ¼ cups (250 g) granulated white sugar
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
2 tablespoons lemon juice
1 tablespoon lemon zest
1 large egg (room temperature)
Icing:
2 cups (228 g) confectioners sugar
3-4 tablespoons lemon juice
2 teaspoons lemon zest
½ teaspoon vanilla extract (optional)
recipes.directions:
Preheat and prepare cooking sheets. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mat (or lightly grease with non-sticking cooking spray).
Whisk dry ingredients together. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. 3 cups (360 g) all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon baking powder ½ teaspoon baking soda ½ teaspoon salt
Cream butter and sugar. In a large bowl using an electric mixer, cream together butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 2 to 3 minutes. 1 cup (2 sticks, 226 g) unsalted butter, 1 ¼ cups (250 g) granulated white sugar
Add remaining wet ingredients. Add egg and beat to combine. Add vanilla, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Beat to combine. Scrape down the bowl as needed. 1 large egg 1 teaspoon vanilla extract 2 tablespoons lemon juice 1 tablespoon lemon zest
Add dry ingredients. Add flour mixture, and mix at low to medium speed just until no flour streaks remain.
Scoop cookie dough. Using a cookie scoop, or tablespoon to portion into 1 ½ inch balls. Place on prepared baking sheets, leaving about 2 inches between them.
Bake and cool the cookies. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes. The edges will look done, but the center will still look slightly soft. Remove from the oven and cool on the baking sheets for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to finish cooling completely.
Make icing. In a small mixing bowl, combine icing ingredients. With an offset spatula, ice the top of each cookie. Adjust consistency if needed. 2 cups (228 g) confectioners sugar 3-4 tablespoons lemon juice 2 teaspoons lemon zest ½ teaspoon vanilla extract

recipes.title: Soft and Chewy Sugar Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
2½ cups (318 grams) all-purpose flour
2 teaspoons baking powder
3/4 teaspoon fine sea salt
2 sticks (226 grams) unsalted butter, at cool room temperature
1¼ cups (250 grams) granulated sugar, plus ¼ cup (50 grams) for rolling
1 large egg plus 1 egg yolk
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
recipes.directions:
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt to combine.
In a large bowl, use an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment on medium-high speed to beat the butter and 1¼ cups sugar until light and fluffy, 2 to 3 minutes. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the mixing bowl. Add the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla, and beat until combined, scraping the bowl down as needed. Slowly beat in the flour mixture.
Place the remaining 1/4 cup sugar in a shallow dish. Using a large (3-tablespoon) spring-loaded scoop, divide the dough into balls, then roll in sugar to coat evenly. Place the dough balls on the prepared baking sheets, spacing 2 inches apart, and flatten slightly with the bottom of a measuring cup.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the cookies set and begin to brown. Cool for 5 minutes before removing to a wire rack to cool completely. Cookies can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.
recipes.notes: If you prefer a thicker cookie, chill the dough balls prior to baking.

recipes.title: Frosted Sugar Cookie Bars
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup unsalted butter (2 sticks) softened
1 cup granulated sugar
1 large egg
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
1 teaspoon almond extract
1 ½ teaspoons baking powder
½ teaspoon salt omit if using salted butter
2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
Buttercream Frosting:
½ cup unsalted butter (1 stick) softened
2 cups powdered sugar
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
½ teaspoon almond extract
1-2 tablespoons milk
Food coloring optional
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 350℉.
In a large bowl, cream together softened unsalted butter and granulated sugar on medium speed for 20-30 seconds. While the mixer is still on medium speed, add in egg, vanilla extract and almond extract.
Once mixed, add in baking powder, salt, and *all-purpose flour ½ cup at a time. When pinched between your fingers, dough should be similar to a playdoh consistency and not be very sticky.
Spray a metal 9×13 pan with cooking spray, then line the pan with parchment paper (having the grease underneath the paper will help the paper stay down).
Drop the dough into 1 tablespoon sized chunks into the prepared pan. Use your hands or a spatula to press down the dough until it covers the pan.
Bake bars for 14-16 minutes. The trick to soft sugar cookie bars is to take them out right when you see the TINIEST amount of brown around the edges. The center may look underdone and this is okay; the bars will continue to bake in the hot pan when cooling.
Allow to cool completely before frosting (you can put the pan in the fridge on a cooling rack to speed this up).
Frosting:
In a large bowl, beat softened unsalted butter on medium speed for 1-2 minutes (until light and creamy).
Keeping the mixer on medium speed, slowly add in vanilla extract, almond extract, and powdered sugar. Once incorporated, turn the mixer to high speed and beat for 2 minutes until light and fluffy. Add milk to desired consistency
recipes.notes: If not eating the same day or within 3-4 hours, place in an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for 2-3 days. These bars are also amazingly frozen for 1-2 months (any longer and they tend to dry out).

recipes.title: Sugar Cookie Cutouts from Grandma Shuty
recipes.ingredients:
2/3 cup butter, softened
3/4 cup granulated sugar
1 teaspoon baking powder
1/4 teaspoon salt
1 egg
1 tablespoon milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
2 cups flour
Powdered Sugar Icing
1 cup sifted powdered sugar
¼ tsp vanilla
1 tbsp milk
recipes.directions:
Beat butter with an electric mixer on medium to high speed for 30 seconds.  Add granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt.  Beat until combined.  Beat in egg, milk and vanilla until combined.  Beat in as much flour as you can with a mixer.  Stir in any remaining flour.  Divide dough in half.  If necessary, cover and chill dough 30 minutes or until easy to handle.
On a lightly floured surface, roll half the dough at a time until 1/8” thick.  Using a cookie cutter, cut dough into desired shapes.  Place 1” apart on an ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake 375* oven for 7 to 8 minutes or until edges are firm and bottoms are very lightly browned.  Transfer to a wire rack and let cool.  If desired, frost with Powdered Sugar Icing.

recipes.title: Moon Cookies from MarMar
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup soft shortening or butter
1/3 cup sugar
2/3 cup ground blanched almonds or pecans or almond flour
1 2/3 cup flour
¼ tsp salt
recipes.directions:
Mix together the ingredients and chill
Roll into pencil thick 2 ½” lengths
Bake on an ungreased baking sheet at 325 degrees for 14-16 minutes or until set, not brown. Cool on pan until warm.
Roll in a mixture of 1 cup confectioners sugar and 1 tsp cinnamon

recipes.title: Jan Hagels
recipes.ingredients:
2 cups flour
2/3 cup ground almonds or almond flour (or flour)
½ tsp baking soda
¼ tsp salt
1 tsp cinnamon
1 cup butter
1 cup brown sugar
1 tsp almond extract
1 small egg beaten
1 tsp cold water
½ cup sliced almonds
recipes.directions:
Knead butter and brown sugar.
Add almond extract.
Mix in remaining dry ingredients.
Press dough onto ungreased cookie sheet
Beat egg and water; brush over dough
Sprinkle with almonds
Bake at 350° for 15-17 minutes.
Cool 2 minutes
Cut into squares.

recipes.title: Twix Thumbprint Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
Shortbread Layer:
2/3 cup Unsalted Butter softened
1/2 cup sugar
2 Eggs yolks only
1 teaspoon vanilla
1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
Caramel Filling:
14 soft caramel candies like Kraft Caramels
3 tablespoon heavy cream
Chocolate Drizzle:
¾ cup milk chocolate chips
recipes.directions:
To make the shortbread layer:
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a medium sized bowl, combine butter, sugar, egg yolks and vanilla. Beat at medium speed, scraping bowl often, until creamy. Add flour; beat at low speed until well mixed. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour or until firm.
Shape dough into 1-inch balls. Place 2 inches apart onto ungreased cookie sheets. Make indentation in the center of each cookie with thumb or end of a wooden spoon handle.
Bake for 7-10 minutes or until the edges begin to brown. Cool completely.
To make the caramel filling:
Melt the caramel and heavy cream in the microwave stirring every 30 seconds until it is fully melted. Using a teaspoon, fill each indentation with the caramel.
To make the chocolate drizzle:
Melt the chocolate in the microwave, stirring 30 seconds at a time until smooth, and scoop into a ziplock bag. Barely snip the corner and drizzle over the cookies.
recipes.notes: Makes 24 cookies

recipes.title: Carrot Cake Cookies with Bourbon
recipes.ingredients:
2 ¾ cup + 1-2 tbsp all purpose flour*
1 tsp baking soda
¾ tsp salt
2 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp ginger
½ tsp nutmeg
1 cup butter, softened
¾ cup brown sugar
¾ cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla extract
2-3 tbsp bourbon*
2 large eggs
1 ¼ cup finely grated carrots
Cream Cheese Frosting:
4 tbsp butter softened
2 oz cream cheese
3 cups powdered sugar
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 tbsp bourbon
4-6 tbsp milk, as needed
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 350°F
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, ginger and nutmeg
In a large bowl, combine butter and sugars until light and fluffy (2-3 min)
Mix in vanilla, bourbon and eggs. Stir in dry ingredients slowly until just incorporated
Drop spoonfuls of cookie batter (~2 tbsp) onto the baking sheet. Bake for 10-12 min until the edges begin to brown and the top looks dry. Cool on baking sheet for 5 min then move to cooling rack
Frosting:
Combine butter and cream cheese.
Gradually mix in powdered sugar 1 cup at a time
Mix in vanilla and bourbon. Adding in milk 1 tbsp at a time until desired consistency
recipes.notes:
This batter is very sticky so will not be rolled into balls
The extra tbsp of flour is dependent on how much bourbon is added and add bourbon to taste

recipes.title: Soft Almond Sugar Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
10 tbsp unsalted butter, softened
¾ cup granulated sugar
1 large egg (room temp)
2 tsp almond extract *
2 cups all purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
½ tsp salt
Icing:
¾ cup powdered sugar
1 ½ tbsp milt
⅛ tsp almond extract
¼ cup sliced almonds (optional, for topping)
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 350°F
In a large bowl, cream together butter and sugar with a mixer on medium speed until light and fluffy (1-2 min). Mix in egg and almond extract
Stir in flour, baking soda, and salt until just combined
Scoop 3-4 tbsp of dough, roll into balls. Bake until edges start to brown (~9-12 min) and cool on the pan for 5 min and then transfer to a cooling rack
Icing:
In a small bowl, mix together powdered sugar, milk and almond extract until smooth
Spread icing over cooled cookies and sprinkle on sliced almonds
recipes.notes: For a more subtle almond taste use 1 tsp of almond extract

recipes.title: Mint Chip Ice Cream Cookies (Crumbl Copycat)
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup unsalted butter
1 cup granulated sugar
1 tsp vanilla extract
¼ tsp mint extract
2 large eggs
3 cups all purpose flour
½ tsp salt
1 ¾ tsp baking powder
½ tsp baking soda
¾ cup mini chocolate chips
Mint Mousse:
1 packet (3.4 oz) vanilla instant pudding mix
2 cups heavy cream
⅔ cups powdered sugar
1 tsp vanilla extract
¾ tsp mint extract
3-4 drops green food coloring
½ cup mini chocolate chips
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven 350°F
Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy (2-3 min). Mix in eggs, vanilla extract and mint extract until well combined. Stir in flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda until just combined. Stir in chocolate chips
Roll dough into balls and flatten on a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Bake for 10-12 min. Leave on the baking sheet for a couple minutes before moving to a cooling rack.
Mint mousse:
Mix dry vanilla instant pudding mix, heavy cream, powdered sugar, vanilla extract, mint extract, and food coloring until smooth and thickened
Frost cookies and enjoy!

recipes.title: Cake Batter Cookies / Birthday Cake Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
1 ½ cups salted butter, Softened
1 ½ cups granulated sugar
3 eggs
1 ½ tsp cake batter flavoring
½ tsp vanilla extract
4 ½ cups all purpose flour
3 tsp baking powder
¼ cup sprinkles (optional)
Cream Cheese Frosting:
8 oz cream cheese
¼ cup butter
½ tsp cake batter flavoring
4 cups powdered sugar
milk (to thin, as needed)
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 350°F
Cream together butter and sugar
Mix in eggs and cake batter flavoring
Mix in flour and baking powder until just combined. Add sprinkles
Roll 1/3 cup dough into balls and flatten
Bake 14-17 min, centers should rise
Frosting:
Whip butter and cream cheese until smooth. Add in cake batter flavoring
Mix in powdered sugar slowly
If needed, use milk to thin frosting
Frost cookies and add sprinkles. Enjoy!
recipes.notes: Buttercream frosting also works great with these cookies

recipes.title: Snickerdoodle Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup unsalted butter, softened
1 ½ cup sugar
2 large eggs
2 tsp vanilla
2 ¾ cup all purpose flour
1 ½ tsp cream of tartar (1 tsp for less tang)
½ tsp baking soda
¾ tsp salt
Cinnamon Sugar mix:
¼ cup sugar
1 ½ tbsp cinnamon
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven 350°F
In a large bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy (2-3 min). Add eggs and vanilla, cream another 1-2 min.
Stir in flour, cream of tartar, baking soda, and salt until just combined
Wrap dough and chill/refrigerate 20-30 min
In a small bowl, mix together sugar and cinnamon
Roll into small balls until smooth, coat in cinnamon sugar mix and place on baking sheet (for flatter cookies press down before putting in the oven)
Bake for 9-11 min, cool for 5 minutes on sheet before moving to a cooling rack
recipes.notes: They are done when the outside is firm and the inside is still gooey, they will finish baking on the sheet out of the oven

recipes.title: Chocolate Chip Cookies (updated Betty Crocker)
recipes.ingredients:
2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
½ tsp baking powder
½ tsp salt
1 cup butter, softened
¾ cups granulated sugar
¾ cups packed brown sugar
1 egg
1 tsp vanilla
1 cup semi sweet chocolate chips
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 375°F
In a small bowl mix flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat softened butter and sugars on medium speed until light and fluffy
Beat in egg and vanilla until smooth. Stir in dry ingredients until just incorporated. Stir in chocolate chips
Scoop dough onto an ungreased baking sheet, bake for 8-10 min or until lightly brown. Cool for 2 min on pan before transferring to cooling rack
recipes.notes: Baking from frozen in toaster oven: 300°F for 5-7 min, for a crispy brown bottom 325°F for 5-7min

recipes.title: Cinnamon Roll Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup (222g) salted butter, softened
1 1/4 cup (288g) granulated sugar
1 egg
2 tbsp light corn syrup
1 tsp vanilla extract
2 1/2 cups (380g) all-purpose flour
1/2 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp salt
Cinnamon Filling:
1/4 cup (56g) butter, partially melted
1/2 cup (123g) brown sugar
1 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1 1/2 tbsp all-purpose flour
Toppings:
¼ cup (50g) white chocolate
Or icing (powdered sugar, milk, vanilla extract)
recipes.directions:
Preheat the oven to 350° F (180°C).
In a small bowl, combine the ingredients in the ‘cinnamon filling’, mixing until smooth. Spread the mixture onto a baking sheet with parchment paper until about 1/4 inch thick. Place the pan in the freezer until needed.
In the bowl of a stand mixer or with an electric hand mixer, cream together the butter and granulated sugar until light in color and fluffy in texture.
Scrape the sides of the bowl and add in the egg, corn syrup and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined.
Add the flour, baking soda and salt. Mix until a soft dough forms.
Remove the cinnamon filling from the freezer and chop into 1/4-1/2 inch squares.
Add the frozen cinnamon filling to the mixer and pulse the mixer just a few times to distribute the cinnamon filling pieces in the dough. Do not mix so much that everything is completely combined, we want there to be swirls and chunks of cinnamon.
Portion out the cookie dough using a cookie scoop for uniform sized cookies.
Bake at 350° F until the cookies spread, flatten and are no longer glossy on top. approx 8-11 minutes for small cookies.
Remove from the oven, and allow the cookies to cool on the pan.
Once the cookies have cooled, melt your white chocolate chips in the microwave. Microwave for 30 seconds and then stir. Repeat until melted.
Transfer the melted chocolate chips to a zip top bag, snip the corner and drizzle the white chocolate onto the cookies.
recipes.notes: Add 1 tsp cream of tartar for a more tangy base

recipes.title: Funfetti Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup (226 g) unsalted butter, melted and cooled until no longer warm to the touch
1 ⅔ cup (333 g) granulated sugar
2 large eggs, room temperature preferred
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
3 cups (375 g) all-purpose flour
1 Tablespoon cornstarch
1 teaspoon baking powder
¾ teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon table salt
⅓ cup sprinkles, nonpareils, quins, jimmies, or a mix
recipes.directions:
Combine melted, cooled butter with sugar and stir until combined.
Add eggs and vanilla extract, stir well.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, baking powder, baking soda, and salt until thoroughly combined.
Gradually stir dry ingredients into wet until completely combined.
Stir in sprinkles until well-distributed through the dough.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes and up to 5 days (dough chilled longer than a few hours may need to sit at room temperature for a bit to become soft enough to scoop easily).
When nearly ready to bake, preheat the oven to 350F (175C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Scoop dough by rounded 1 ½ Tablespoon-sized scoops and drop onto a baking sheet (or roll the dough into smooth balls between your palms for rounder, more uniform cookies) spacing cookies at least 2” (5cm) apart.
Transfer baking sheet to 350F (175C) preheated oven and bake cookies for 10-12 minutes. Cookies may look slightly underdone when they come out of the oven, this is fine and makes them nice and chewy as they continue to cook on the baking sheet as they cool. Let them cool completely on the baking sheet before removing as they’re fragile when warm!
recipes.notes: Baking from frozen: small cookies - 275 for 5 min

recipes.title: Cookies and Cream Pie (Oreo)
recipes.ingredients:
Crust:
24 whole Oreos (Double Stuf or regular)
5 Tablespoons (72g) unsalted butter, melted
Filling:
1 cup (240ml) cold heavy cream or heavy whipping cream
one 8-ounce block (225g) full-fat cream cheese, softened to room temp
3/4 cup (90g) confectioners’ sugar
1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
12 whole Oreos (Double Stuf or regular), chopped
optional for topping: whipped cream
recipes.directions:
Preheat to 350°F (177°C).
Make the crust: In a food processor or blender, pulse the whole Oreos (cream filling and cookie) into a fine crumb. You should have about 2 cups. Stir the Oreo crumbs and melted butter together. Press tightly into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch pie dish. Bake for 10 minutes. Allow to cool as you prepare the filling.
Make the filling: Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whip the cold heavy cream into stiff peaks on medium-high speed, about 4 minutes. Set aside. Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk or paddle attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium speed until perfectly smooth and creamy. Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl with a rubber spatula as needed. Add the confectioners’ sugar and vanilla extract and beat on medium-high speed until combined. Make sure there are no large lumps of cream cheese. If there are lumps, keep beating until smooth.
Using your mixer on low speed or a rubber spatula, fold the whipped cream and chopped Oreos into the cream cheese mixture until combined. This takes several turns of your rubber spatula. Combine slowly as you don’t want to deflate all the air in the whipped cream.
Spread filling into cooled crust. Use a spatula to smooth down the top.
Cover tightly with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 6 hours and up to 2 days. The longer it is refrigerated, the better the pie will set up.

recipes.title: Bonket from Grandma Monsma
recipes.ingredients:
Pastry:
1 lb. Butter
1 lb. Flour (4 cups)
1 cup water
Mix these ingredients.  Let stand overnight so the crust is firm.
Filling:
12 oz. Almond paste (I use 8 oz sometimes)
1 lb. Sugar (2 cups)
3 eggs (beaten)
1 1/2 tsp. Almond extract
Mix thoroughly and let stand overnight.
2 cups almond flour (or grind up blanched almonds)
1 cup powdered sugar
¼ tsp salt
1 egg white
1 ½ tsp almond extract
recipes.directions:
When ready to bake, divide into 8-12 parts.  Roll out each piece of crust with flour. Roll each part into a long oval shape with flour. (Put flour on a clean surface and spread it out.) Put in the filling. Spread the filling in a long line. Lap crust over each other. Fold the ends of the oval over the filling then do the sides.  Brush beaten egg white (just until foamy) on the inside of the crust and top. Punch holes in the top of the crust every 2 inches with a fork.
Bake on a greased (or use parchment paper) cookie sheet at 400 degrees for 14-18 minutes.  Watch carefully so that they do not get too brown. Or else the filling gets too hard.
Almond Paste:
Add almond flour, powdered sugar and salt in a bowl until evenly mixed
Add egg white and almond extract until you have a smooth paste

recipes.title: Macaron
recipes.ingredients:
100 g egg whites room temperature 3 large eggs
140 g almond flour 1 1/2 cups
90 g granulated sugar just under 1/2 cup
130 g powdered sugar 1 cup
1 tsp vanilla 5mL
1/4 tsp cream of tartar 800mg
recipes.directions:
Sift the confectioners sugar and almond flour into a bowl.
Add the room temperature egg whites into a very clean bowl.
Using an electric mixer, whisk egg whites. Once they begin to foam add the cream of tartar and then SLOWLY add the granulated sugar.
Add the food coloring (if desired) and vanilla then mix in. Continue to beat until stiff peaks form.
Begin folding in the 1/3 of the dry ingredients.
Be careful to add the remaining dry ingredients and fold gently.
The final mixture should look like flowing lava, and be able to fall into a figure eight without breaking. Spoon into a piping bag with a medium round piping tip and you’re ready to start piping.
Pipe one inch dollops onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper (this should be glued down with dabs of batter). Tap on counter several times to release air bubbles. Allow to sit for about 40 minutes before placing in oven.
Bake at 300F for 12-15 minutes, rotate tray after 7 minutes. Allow to cool completely before removing from baking sheet.
recipes.notes:
*try baking 325F for 12 min (no rotate)
*be sure to fully sift flour and powdered sugar

recipes.title: Creamy Vanilla Frosting
recipes.ingredients:
3 cups powdered sugar
⅓ cup butter, softened
1 ½ teaspoon vanilla
About 2 tablespoons milk
recipes.directions:
Mix powdered sugar and butter.
Stir in vanilla and milk.
Beat until smooth and of spreading consistency.
recipes.notes:
Frosts a 13x9-inch cake or fills and fronts two 8 or 9 inch cake layers.
To fill and frost 3 8-inch layers, use 4 ½ cups powdered sugar, ½ cup butter, softened, 2 teaspoons vanilla and about 3 tablespoons milk.

recipes.title: Penuche Frosting
recipes.ingredients:
1/2 cup (8 tablespoons) unsalted butter
1 cup brown sugar, packed
1/4 cup milk
2 cups sifted confectioners' sugar, more or less
Hot water, optional
recipes.directions:
In a saucepan, melt 1/2 cup butter. Add the brown sugar. Bring to a boil and lower heat to medium-low. Boil for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the milk and bring back to a boil, stirring constantly.
Remove the pan from the heat and let the mixture cool to lukewarm.
Gradually add sifted confectioners' sugar while beating until thick enough to spread. If it's too thick, add a little hot water.
Makes enough frosting to cover the top and sides of a 2-layer cake or a 13 x 9-inch cake.

recipes.title: Oreo Balls Oreo Truffles
recipes.ingredients:
36 Oreos (original not double stuff), plus 3 more, crushed, for topping if desired
1 (8 oz) pkg. cream cheese, softened
16 oz vanilla or chocolate candy melts, or melted white chocolate, milk chocolate or dark chocolate
recipes.directions:
Line a plate(s) or pan with wax paper.
Place 36 Oreos in a food processor or blender (entire cookie, don't remove cream filling) and crush to fine crumbs. Can also do this by hand in a zip lock baggie.
Add cream cheese and mix in bursts until well combined. Or can do this in a mixing bowl with a mixer. Should be “muddy” looking when done.
Scoop mixture out about 1 Tbsp at a time and form into 1 inch balls then place on a prepared cookie sheet.
Place truffles in the freezer for 15 minutes. Meanwhile crush the remaining 3 Oreos.
Melt choice of chocolate according to directions on the package of chocolate.
Remove truffles from the freezer and dip in melted chocolate. Can use a fork or other choice of utensil and mix the Oreo ball in chocolate until completely covered.
Return to the baking sheet, immediately sprinkle tops with crushed Oreos if desired, then allow chocolate to set.
Freeze for 1 hour then serve, or can store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
recipes.notes: Can be left out at room temperature for up to 2 hours while serving. Best kept in the refrigerator. Can store in the freezer for a couple months.

recipes.title: Edible Cookie Dough
recipes.ingredients:
¼ cup + 1 tbsp all-purpose flour (heat treated)
2 tbsp butter softened (or melt)
1 tbsp granulated sugar
2 tbsp packed brown sugar
½ tbsp milk
¼ tsp vanilla
1 pinch salt
2 tbsp chocolate chips
recipes.directions:
Soften or melt butter in a bowl
Add flour, sugars, milk and salt and mix together
Fold in chocolate chips
recipes.notes: Makes 2 large servings

recipes.title: Chocolate Chip Cookie Dip
recipes.ingredients:
8 oz (1 block) cream cheese, softened
½ cup butter, softened
¼ cup packed brown sugar
⅓ cup sugar
2 tsp vanilla extract
1 cup mini chocolate chips
recipes.directions:
Mix cream cheese and butter with sugars until light and fluffy.
Mix in vanilla extract
Fold in mini chocolate chips

recipes.title: Chocolate Fudge
recipes.ingredients:
2 tbsp salted butter
3 cups chocolate chips
14 oz sweetened condensed milk
1 tsp vanilla extract (optional) *
recipes.directions:
In a medium saucepan, combine the butter, chocolate chips and sweetened condensed milk.
Place the pan over medium low heat. Stir constantly until the chocolate is partially melted. Stir in the vanilla extract.
Continue stirring until the chocolate is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and starts to turn shiny.
Pour the mixture into the prepared pan. It will be very thick – if it doesn't pour well because of the thickness, don't stress, you're still on the right track. Once it is all in the pan, you can gently shake the pan back and forth to help it settle. You can also use a rubber spatula to spread it.
Allow the fudge to completely set. This can be done on the countertop and will take about four hours.
Remove the fudge from the pan and take off the aluminum foil. Trim off the rounded edges from the pan. Cut the fudge in half one way and then the other to make four equal squares. Cut each small square into 9 equal pieces by cutting into thirds one way and then the other. This will result in 36 equal pieces of fudge.
Enjoy right away or store in an airtight container for up to two weeks. (See the post for instructions on freezing.)
recipes.notes:
This recipe can be easily adjusted to have different flavors. Use different chocolate or extracts
Can add add-ins (ex: oreos) but do this as the last step right before putting in pan for cooling

recipes.title: Chewy Caramels
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup butter (2 sticks)
4 cups granulated sugar
2 cups light corn syrup
24 ounces evaporated milk* (2 cans)
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
recipes.directions:
Line a 9×13” pan or jelly roll pan with parchment paper. (If you don’t have parchment paper you can generously butter the pan.) Either size pan will work–9×13” will yield thicker caramels.
Add 2 sticks butter, sugar, and karo syrup to a large heavy-bottom saucepan over medium heat. Stir over medium heat until mixture begins to boil, about 5-10 minutes.
Gradually add the evaporated milk, one can at a time, taking about 12-15 minutes PER CAN to slowly add it, while stirring constantly. You want to make sure the mixture maintains a steady heat and constant boil (no drastic changes in temperature) otherwise your caramels can curdle.
Stir the mixture constantly, scraping the sides occasionally until it reaches a firm ball stage (about 240-245 degrees F on a candy thermometer). It takes patience and time, but it's sooo worth it! (Also, I don't really trust or rely on a candy thermometer–I like to test it the old fashioned way. Drop a spoonful of hot caramel sauce into a cup of ice water and mold it with your fingers into a ball. When ready it will feel pretty firm and pliable, but still slightly sticky.)
Once you reach 240-245 degrees F / or the firm ball stage, remove from heat. Stir in vanilla.
Pour caramels into the prepared pan. Refrigerate until cooled and hardened. (Best if you refrigerate them overnight, or for several hours. They will be easier to cut and wrap).
The caramel will seem hard in the fridge after they're set, but they should be soft at room temperature. Cut caramel into small pieces and, if desired, wrap like a tootsie roll in wax paper.
recipes.notes: ½ of this recipe makes a lot depending on size of caramel desired

recipes.title: Rice Krispie Treats
recipes.ingredients:
6 cups Rice Krispies
6 tbsp salted butter
1.5 mini marshmallow bag
1- 2 tsp vanilla (to taste)
recipes.directions:
Brown butter in large pot
Add one bag of mini marshmallows and melt completely
Add vanilla and stir to combine
Add ½ marshmallow bag, stir to incorporate but don’t fully melt
Add Rice Krispie cereal and stir to combine
If desired add sprinkles or mini chocolate chips now
Pour into pan, and allow time to set before cutting
recipes.notes: Can use other extract flavors

recipes.title: Zucchini Bread
recipes.ingredients:
3 cups shredded zucchini (2 to 3 medium)
1 2/3 cups sugar
2/3 cup vegetable oil
2 teaspoons vanilla
4 eggs
3 cups all-purpose or whole wheat flour
2 teaspoons baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
1 teaspoon ground cloves
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
2/3 cup coarsely chopped nuts
2/3 cup raisins, if desired
recipes.directions:
1.       Move oven rack to low position so that tops of pans will be in center of oven. Heat oven to 350°F. Grease bottoms only of 2 (8x4-inch) loaf pans or 1 (9x5-inch) loaf pan with shortening or cooking spray.
2.       In large bowl, stir zucchini, sugar, oil, vanilla and eggs until well mixed. Stir in remaining ingredients except nuts and raisins. Stir in nuts and raisins. Divide batter evenly between 8-inch pans or pour into 9-inch pan.
3.       Bake 8-inch loaves 50 to 60 minutes, 9-inch loaf 1 hour 10 minutes to 1 hour 20 minutes, or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pans on cooling rack 10 minutes.
4.       Loosen sides of loaves from pans; remove from pans and place top side up on cooling rack. Cool completely, about 2 hours, before slicing. Wrap tightly and store at room temperature up to 4 days, or refrigerate up to 10 days.
Prep 15 min  Total 3 hr 25 min
recipes.notes:
Pumpkin Bread
Follow the recipe for Zucchini Bread but substitute 1 (15-oz.) can pure pumpkin purée for the zucchini.
From Betty Crocker Kitchens

recipes.title: Waldorf Salad
recipes.ingredients:
apples
celery
walnuts
raisins
mayonnaise
confectioners sugar
recipes.directions:
Cut up apples and celery.
Mix in walnuts and raisins.
Dress in mayonnaise or sprinkle with confectioners’ sugar.

recipes.title: Turkey Apple-Raisin Bread Stuffing
recipes.ingredients:
¾ cup butter or margarine
2 large celery stalks, chopped (1 ½ cup)
1 medium onion, chopped (3/4 cup)
9 cups soft bread cubes
1 ½ teaspoons chopped fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme leaves
1 1/2 teaspoon salt
½ teaspoon ground sage
¼ teaspoon pepper
3 cups finely chopped apples
¾ cup raisins
recipes.directions:
Melt butter in 4-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Cook celery and onion in butter 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender. Remove Dutch oven from the heat.
Gently toss celery mixture and remaining ingredients, using spoon, until bread cubes are evenly coated.
Use to stuff one 10- to 12-pound turkey. Or to bake stuffing separately, grease 3-quart casserole or rectangular baking dish, 13x9x2 inches. Place stuffing in casserole or baking dish. Cover with lid or aluminum foil and bake at 325°F for 30 minutes; uncover and bake 15 minutes longer.
recipes.notes: From Betty Crocker, 1986

recipes.title: Tuna Casserole
recipes.ingredients:
1-2 slices of onion, diced
2 cans of cream of mushroom soup (15 oz)
4 slices of American cheese (can use other kinds of cheese)
1+ cup milk
¼ tsp dry mustard
1 cup peas
2 cans of tuna
2-3 cups dry macaroni
recipes.directions:
Heat oven to 350 degrees
Boil water for noodles; Cook 1 minute less than directions
Sauté onions in a large skillet
Add milk and soup; stir and heat
Add cheese and mustard; stir when starts to melt
Add tuna, peas and noodles
Put in 2 qt casserole dish
Bake ½ hour

recipes.title: Tilapia Parmesan Sauté
recipes.ingredients:
4-5 Tilapia Filets
1 tbsp olive oil
 ¼ cup grated parmesan cheese
2 tbsp parsley flakes
2 tbsp butter
1 tbsp lemon juice
Garlic powder
recipes.directions:
Heat a large sauté pan and add olive oil (I usually skip it), butter and lemon (I often skip this too).  Sometimes I sauté minced garlic.
Sauté fillets 2-3 minutes per side until white and flakey.
Sprinkle cheese, garlic and parsley (I usually forget about this) on fillets and sauté for another minute.
Then serve.

recipes.title: Stuffed Peppers
recipes.ingredients:
4  large bell peppers (any color)
1 lb lean (at least 80%) ground beef
2 tablespoons chopped onion
1 cup cooked rice
1 teaspoon salt
1 clove garlic, finely chopped
1 can (15 oz) tomato sauce
¾ cup shredded mozzarella cheese (3 oz)
recipes.directions:
Enjoy your dinner with peppers that are stuffed with ground beef and baked to perfection.
Cut a thin slice from the stem end of each bell pepper to remove the top of pepper. Remove seeds and membranes; rinse peppers. If necessary, cut a thin slice from the bottom of each pepper so they stand up straight. In 4-quart Dutch oven, add enough water to cover peppers. Heat to boiling; add peppers. Cook for about 5 minutes; drain.
In a 10-inch skillet, cook beef and onion over medium heat 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until beef is brown; drain. Stir in rice, salt, garlic and 1 cup of the tomato sauce; cook until hot.
Heat oven to 350°F.
Stuff peppers with beef mixture. Stand peppers upright in an ungreased 8-inch square glass baking dish. Pour remaining tomato sauce over peppers.
Cover tightly with foil. Bake for 45 minutes. Uncover and bake about 15 minutes longer or until peppers are tender. Sprinkle with cheese.
recipes.notes: From Betty Crocker, 1986

recipes.title: Spinach Salad
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup vegetable oil
¾ cup sugar
¼ cup vinegar
1 tsp. salt
1/3 cup ketchup
1 small grated onion
1 tbsp. worcestershire sauce
recipes.directions:
Mix the above ingredients in the blender for a minute or so.
Toss some in the salad below and serve the rest.
½ lb. bacon
1 lb. fresh spinach
4 boiled eggs
1 can water chestnuts
1 can sprouts

recipes.title: Spicy Burgundy Chicken
recipes.ingredients:
2 tbsp oil
1 – 2 ½ pound chicken pieces
1 jar 15 ounces marinara
1/3 cup burgundy or dry red wine
2 large garlic cloves minced
1 medium onion sliced
1 red pepper sliced optional
8 oz mushrooms sliced optional
recipes.directions:
In a large skillet heat oil on medium high heat. Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Brown chicken in the pan for 3 minutes per side, just until browned.
Add onions, peppers, and mushrooms to pan. Pour in the marinara sauce, wine and garlic and stir to combine.
Let simmer until chicken is cooked through to an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit, about 15 - 30 minutes. Taste and add salt and pepper as desired.
 Serve hot and garnish with chopped parsley and parmesan cheese, optional.

recipes.title: Sloppy Joes
recipes.ingredients:
1 pound ground beef
1 medium onion, chopped (about ½ cup)
1/3 cup chopped celery
1/3 cup chopped red pepper
1 cup ketchup
¼ cup water
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
1 tsp salt
1/8 tsp red pepper sauce
6 hamburger buns, split and toasted
recipes.directions:
Cook and stir ground beef and onion in 10-inch skillet until beef is brown; drain.
Stir in remaining ingredients except buns.
Cover and cook over low heat just until vegetables are tender 10 to 15 minutes.
Fill buns with beef mixture

recipes.title: Shrimp Scampi with Linguini
recipes.ingredients:
1 pound linguini
4 tablespoons butter
4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
2 shallots, finely diced  (can substitute with more garlic)
2 cloves garlic, minced
Pinch red pepper flakes, optional
1 pound shrimp, peeled and deveined
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1/2 cup dry white wine
Juice of 1 lemon
1/4 cup finely chopped parsley leaves
recipes.directions:
For the pasta, put a large pot of water on the stove to boil. 
When it has come to the boil, add a couple of tablespoons of salt and the linguini. Stir to make sure the pasta separates; cover. 
When the water returns to a boil, cook for about 6 to 8 minutes or until the pasta is not quite done. Drain the pasta.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons butter in 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Saute the shallots, garlic, and red pepper flakes (if using) until the shallots are translucent, about 3 to 4 minutes. 
Season the shrimp with salt and pepper; add them to the pan and cook until they have turned pink, about 2 to 3 minutes. 
Remove the shrimp from the pan; set aside and keep warm. Add wine and lemon juice and bring to a boil. 
Add 2 tablespoons butter and 2 tablespoons oil. When the butter has melted, return the shrimp to the pan along with the parsley and cooked pasta. 
Stir well and season with salt and pepper. 
Drizzle over a bit more olive oil and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Scalloped Potatoes
recipes.ingredients:
4 tablespoons butter
1 small onion, finely chopped (1/3 cup)
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon pepper
2 ½ cups milk
6 medium peeled or unpeeled potatoes, thinly sliced (6 cups)
recipes.directions:
Heat oven to 350°F. Grease 2-quart casserole with shortening or cooking spray.
In 2-quart saucepan, melt 3 tablespoons of the butter over medium heat. Cook onion in butter about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender. Stir in flour, salt and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until smooth and bubbly; remove from heat. Stir in milk. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Boil and stir 1 minute.
Spread potatoes in casserole. Pour sauce over potatoes. Cut remaining 1 tablespoon butter into small pieces; sprinkle over potatoes.
Cover; bake 30 minutes. Uncover; bake 1 hour to 1 hour 10 minutes longer or until potatoes are tender. Let stand 5 to 10 minutes before serving (sauce thickens as it stands).
recipes.notes: From Betty Crocker, 1986

recipes.title: Rita's Meat Balls
recipes.ingredients:
1 lb. beef, pork mixture
½ tsp. Salt
1 egg, beaten
½ cup bread crumbs
¼ tsp. Black pepper
1 tsp. Onion powder
3 Tbsp. grate parmesan cheese
recipes.directions:
Mix thoroughly.  Form into 10 to 12 balls depending on what size you want.  Bake at 350” for 20 minutes or until done.
I usually double or triple the recipe.
recipes.notes: 4 Tbsp = ¼ cup

recipes.title: Rice
recipes.ingredients:
2 cups water
1 teaspoon salt
1 tablespoon butter
1 cup long grain white rice – slightly less is a good idea
recipes.directions:
Bring the water to boil in a medium saucepan. Add the salt and butter and allow the butter to melt.
When the water has returned to a boil, stir in the rice. Let the water return to a light simmer. Stir again, cover the pot and turn the heat down to low. Keep the rice simmering slightly, and keep the pot covered (you may have to peek after a few minutes to make sure the heat is at the correct temperature, but then let it cook, covered). Start checking to see if the rice is tender and all of the liquid is absorbed at about 20 minutes. It may take up to 25, especially if you are making a larger quantity of rice.
When the rice is cooked, turn off the heat and let it sit for at least 5 more minutes to finish absorbing any liquid. (Can leave covered until you are close to serving.) Take off the lid, fluff the rice with a fork and let it sit for another 2 minutes or so, so that some of the excess moisture in the rice dries off.

recipes.title: Chicken in Red Sauce (aka Apricot Chicken)
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup apricot preserves
1 env. dry onion soup mix
½ bottle Russian dressing
8 chicken thighs/legs, skinless
recipes.directions: In a bowl, mix together the apricot jam, onion soup mix, and Russian dressing. Arrange the chicken breasts in an appropriately sized casserole dish or baking dish. Pour the sauce over the chicken and bake covered in a 250-degree oven for 3 hours.
recipes.notes:
This recipe isn’t fussy.  Aunt Linda shared it with me.  I don’t usually measure the jelly or the dressing for example.  You can speed it up by cooking at 300 for less time and so on.  You could probably do it in a crock pot also.
The preserves can be the cheapest you can find.  I’ve also used apricot and pineapple preserves.  Instead of Russian, you can use Catalina.  I like the Russian best.
If you want to use chicken breasts be sure to cook on a higher temp for less time.  I’ve seen 350 for 1 hour recommended.  They also had suggested marinating overnight. But I like the thighs and legs best as the meat is just falling apart.
We usually serve it over egg noodles but fettuccine or rice would work too.
You can also make it in a slow cooker and shred the chicken.

recipes.title: Apricot Ginger Stir Fry
recipes.ingredients:
boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1/2 inch pieces or peeled and deveined shrimp
1-2 tablespoons cooking oil/sesame oil
1 tbsp toasted sesame seeds (1/4 cup cashews)
½ tsp dried ginger
¼ tsp cayenne pepper
¼ tsp black pepper
1 garlic clove, minced
3 tbsp soy sauce (or teriyaki sauce)
3 tbsp apricot preserves
¾ cup water/chicken broth with1/8 cup corn starch
Vegetables such as:
1 medium onion, halved and sliced/ green onions
Red pepper
1 cup celery
1/2 cup snow peas, halved
Broccoli
Carrots
Sugar snap peas
Water chestnuts
Mushrooms
Bamboo shoots
Serve over hot cooked rice
recipes.directions:
Prepare chicken/shrimp.
Stir together sesame seeds, ginger, cayenne pepper, black pepper.  Marinate chicken/shrimp.
In small bowl, whisk together water/broth, corn starch, soy sauce, apricot preserves.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Stir-fry onion, red pepper, celery for 2-3 minutes or until tender.
Add chicken/shrimp and stir-fry 2-3 minutes.
Add remaining vegetables then stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Pour apricot mixture over cooked chicken/shrimp and veges and cook 1 additional minute or until boiling.

recipes.title: Artichoke Dip in Sour Dough Bread
recipes.ingredients:
1 large round loaf sour dough bread
2 Tbsp. butter
1 bunch green onions chopped
6 cloves garlic minced finely
8 oz. (low fat) cream cheese room temperature
16 oz.  (low fat) sour cream
12 oz. grated cheddar cheese
14 oz. can water packed artichoke hearts
drain and cut into eighths
recipes.directions:
Cut a 5 inch hole in top of the bread.
Remove and discard the soft bread.  Save the top part.
Saute onions and garlic in butter.
In a bowl, cut the cream cheese into small chunks.
Add the onions, garlic, sour cream and cheddar cheese.
Mix well.
Fold in the artichokes.
Put the mixture into the bread.  It will be pretty full.
Put the top on the bread and wrap in double thickness foil.
Bake in 350 degree oven for 2 hours.
Serve with Ritz crackers or butter crackers.
recipes.notes: Hope you enjoy.	-- Beth Shuty

recipes.title: au Gratin Potatoes
recipes.ingredients:
6 medium boiling or baking potatoes (2 lb)
¼ cup butter or margarine
1 medium onion, chopped (1/2 cup)
1 tablespoon Gold Medal™ all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon pepper
2 cups milk
2 cups shredded natural sharp Cheddar cheese (8 oz)
¼ cup Progresso™ dry bread crumbs (any flavor)
Paprika
recipes.directions:
Heat oven to 375ºF.
Scrub potatoes but do not peel. Cut into 1/8-inch slices to measure about 4 cups.
Melt butter in 2-quart saucepan over medium heat. Cook onion in butter about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender. Stir in flour, salt and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until bubbly; remove from heat.
Stir in milk and 1 1/2 cups of the cheese. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Boil and stir 1 minute.
Spread potatoes in ungreased 1 1/2-quart casserole. Pour cheese sauce over potatoes.
Bake uncovered 1 hour. Mix remaining cheese and the bread crumbs; sprinkle over potatoes. Sprinkle with paprika. Bake uncovered 15 to 20 minutes longer or until top is brown and bubbly.

recipes.title: Banana Bread
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup sugar
1/3 cup margarine or butter, softened
2 eggs
1 1/2 cups mashed ripe bananas (3 to 4 medium)
1/3 cup water
1 2/3 cups all-purpose flour*
1 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon baking powder
1/2 cup chopped nuts
recipes.directions:
Heat oven to 350°.  Grease bottom only of loaf pan, 8 1/2x4 1/2x2 1/2 or 9x5x3 inches. 
 Mix sugar and margarine in 2 1/2quart bowl.  Stir in eggs until blended.  
Add bananas and water; beat 30 seconds.  Stir in remaining ingredients except nuts just until moistened; stir in nuts.  Pour into pan. 
 Bake until wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean, 8-inch loaf 1 1/4 hours, 9inch loaf 55 to 60 minutes, muffins 25 to 35 min; cool 5 minutes.
  Loosen sides of loaf from pan; remove from pan.  Cool completely before slicing. 1 loaf (24 slices); 120 calories per slice
recipes.notes:
Do not use self-rising flour in this recipe.
Do-ahead Tip:  Wrap and refrigerate no longer than 1 week.  1 loaf (24 slices); 120 calories per slice.

recipes.title: Beef Stew
recipes.ingredients:
2 pounds beef chuck, cubed
1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
¼-1/2 cup flour
1 ½ tsp salt
 ½ tsp pepper
1 tsp paprika
1 ½ cups beef broth
 Half garlic clove minced
1 bay leaf
 4 carrots, sliced
 2 onion, chopped
1 rib celery, sliced
3 potatoes, diced
recipes.directions:
Place meat in slow cooker
Combine flour, salt, pepper paprika.  Stir into meat until coated thoroughly.
Add remaining ingredients.  Mix well.
Cover.  Cook on Low 10-12 hours or High 4-6.  Stir before serving

recipes.title: Beef Stroganoff
recipes.ingredients:
1½ pounds beef cut into cubes
1 tablespoon butter
1 small sweet onion sliced
2 tablespoons butter
¾ lb. sliced mushrooms
Salt and pepper (can wait and do at the table)
A grating of nutmeg
½ cup dry white wine
½ cup warm sour cream
recipes.directions:
Sautee the onions and mushrooms in 2 tablespoons of butter; remove from pan
Brown the beef cubes
Remove any excess liquid
Melt the remaining tablespoon of butter in the pan.  Add the beef, onions and mushrooms.  Add ½ cup of dry white wine along with ¼ teaspoon of nutmeg. Heat until bubbling.
Reduce the liquid by simmering until about ½ liquid is gone (doesn’t really matter but sauce will be thicker if you do)
Turn off the heat.
Add the sour cream just before serving.

recipes.title: Black Eyed Pea Dip
recipes.ingredients:
1 can white corn
1 can black eyed peas
2-3 Roma tomatoes (To save time, I used 1 can of diced tomatoes)
2/3 cup cilantro chopped
1-2 avocados
1/3 cup green onion chopped
¼ cup Italian salad dressing
Blend all ingredients / Served with chips and Frito scoops
Susan Rocco’s version:
3 15 oz. cans (rinsed and drained) black eyed peas
1 bunch of cilantro (snipped or chopped)
3 green onions – chopped
5 avocados – mashed
5 lg. Roma tomatoes – diced
3 jalapeño peppers – chopped finely (She used 1)
1 pkg. Good Seasonings Italian dressing (made to recipe)
recipes.directions: Blend all ingredients / Salt to taste / Serve with chips or Fritos
recipes.notes:
Other versions:
Check the internet with for Black Eye Pea Dip or Cowboy Caviar .
There are tons of variations.

recipes.title: Blueberry Muffins
recipes.ingredients:
Cooking spray to grease muffin cups or 12 paper baking cups
1 cup fresh, frozen or canned blueberries
3/4 cup milk
1/2 cup vegetable oil
1 large egg
2 cups all-purpose flour
⅓ cup granulated sugar
3 teaspoons baking powder
½ teaspoon salt
recipes.directions:
Heat the oven to 400°F. Spray just the bottoms of 12 regular-size muffin cups with the cooking spray, or line each cup with a paper baking cup.
If using canned blueberries, drain them in a strainer. Rinse fresh or canned blueberries with cool water, and discard any crushed ones. Do not thaw frozen blueberries. Pull off any stems from blueberries.
In a large bowl, beat the milk, oil, vanilla and egg with a fork or wire whisk until well mixed. Stir in the flour, sugar, baking powder and salt all at once just until the flour is moistened. The batter will be lumpy. If the batter is mixed too much, the muffins will have high peaks instead of being rounded.
Carefully stir in the blueberries. Spoon the batter into the muffin cups, dividing batter evenly.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown. If baked in a sprayed pan, let stand about 5 minutes in the pan, then remove muffins from pan to a cooling rack. If baked in paper baking cups, immediately remove muffins from the pan to a cooling rack. Serve warm or cool.
recipes.notes: Betty Crocker, 1986

recipes.title: Chicken and Black Bean-Stuffed Burritos
recipes.ingredients:
1/4 cup water
2 tablespoons fresh lime juice
1/2 teaspoon chili powder
1/4 teaspoon ground cumin
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/8 teaspoon ground red pepper
2 cups shredded rotisserie chicken breast
1/4 cup thinly sliced green onions
3/4 cup canned black beans, rinsed and drained
1/2 cup refrigerated fresh salsa
4 (8-inch) flour tortillas
1/2 cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese
Cooking spray
recipes.directions:
Bring first 6 ingredients to a boil in a small saucepan. Stir in shredded chicken and green onions.
Combine beans and salsa. Spoon 1/4 cup bean mixture and 1/2 cup chicken mixture down center of each tortilla; sprinkle with 2 tablespoons cheese. Roll up.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Coat pan with cooking spray. Add 2 burritos. Place a cast-iron or other heavy skillet on top of burritos, and cook for 3 minutes on each side. Remove from pan, and repeat procedure with the remaining 2 burritos.
recipes.notes:
These skillet-grilled burritos come together in a flash thanks to store-bought rotisserie chicken. Keep this meal light by serving with a fresh green salad.
YIELD: 4 servings (serving size: 1 burrito)
TOTAL:25 Minutes
COURSE: Main Dishes

recipes.title: Chicken Parmesan
recipes.ingredients:
4 boneless skinless chicken breast halves
salt and pepper
1⁄2 cup Italian breadcrumbs
1 egg, beaten
4 tablespoons butter
2 cups spaghetti sauce
4 slices mozzarella cheese
1 tablespoon grated parmesan cheese
Basil
recipes.directions:
Pound chicken to flatten (or use thin sliced chicken breast or slice yourself)
Salt and pepper to taste.
Dip chicken in egg; then in bread crumbs.
Fry in butter in hot skillet, turning and browning for 10 minutes or until chicken is done.
Remove from skillet.
To skillet add spaghetti sauce.
Heat thoroughly.
Add chicken.
Sprinkle with Basil
Place slices of mozzarella on top of chicken.
Sprinkle with parmesan.
Cover and cook until cheese is melted.
Serve with a side of spaghetti.

recipes.title: Chicken Pot Pie
recipes.ingredients:
1/3 cup butter
1/3 cup all-purpose flour
1/3 cup chopped onion
½ teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon pepper
1 ¾ cups chicken broth
2/3 cup milk
2 ½ to 3 cups cut-up cooked chicken or turkey
1 bag (10 oz) frozen peas and carrots
1 package (15 oz) refrigerated pie crusts, softened as directed on box
recipes.directions:
In 2-quart saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Stir in flour, onion, salt and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until mixture is bubbly; remove from heat. Stir in broth and milk. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Boil and stir 1 minute. Stir in chicken and peas and carrots; remove from heat.
Heat oven to 425°F. Roll 1 pie crust into 13-inch square. Ease into ungreased 9-inch (2-quart) glass baking dish. Pour chicken mixture into crust-lined dish.
Roll remaining pie crust into 11-inch square. Cut out designs with 1-inch cookie cutter. Place square over chicken mixture. Arrange cutouts on top crust. Turn edges of pie crust under; flute edge.
Bake about 35 minutes or until golden brown.
recipes.notes: From Betty Crocker, 1986 Edition

recipes.title: Chili
recipes.ingredients:
1+ lb. ground beef
Onion
2 can kidney beans
1 can tomato chunks
1 can tomato sauce
1 can tomato paste
1 can corn
Red pepper
Chili powder
Carrots (Chuck adds, Beth doesn’t)
recipes.directions:
Saute onions and brown ground beef.  Drain off fat.
Add cans of stuff.  Add one can of water in the tomato paste can.
Cut up red pepper into small chunks.  Add to sauce.
Add two TABLESPOONS Chili Powder (or whatever looks good).
Let simmer for at least 20 minutes.
Serve over spaghetti pasta with shredded cheese on top

recipes.title: Classic Green Bean Casserole
recipes.ingredients:
1 can Cream of Mushroom Soup
½ cup milk
1 teaspoon soy sauce
1 dash black pepper
4 cups cooked cut green beans
1 1/3cups French's® French Fried Onions
recipes.directions:
Stir the soup, milk, soy sauce, black pepper, beans and 2/3 cup onions in a 1 1/2-quart casserole.

Bake at 350°F. for 25 minutes or until the bean mixture is hot and bubbling.  Stir the bean mixture.  Sprinkle with the remaining onions.

Bake for 5 minutes or until the onions are golden brown.

recipes.title: Creamy Chicken
recipes.ingredients:
1 ¼ pounds skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
1 can Campbell's® Condensed Cream of Mushroom Soup or Campbell's® Condensed 98% Fat Free Cream of Mushroom Soup
1 cup sour cream (exact amount doesn’t matter)
Sliced mushrooms (I use Portabella)
recipes.directions:
Mix soup and 1 cup sour cream together.  Optional: add in sliced mushrooms.
Place the chicken into a 2-quart shallow baking dish.  Spread the soup mixture over the chicken.  Cover the baking dish.
Bake at 350°F. for 25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve over Rice.

recipes.title: Easy Baked Chicken
recipes.ingredients:
8 boneless chicken breasts/thighs     
                         	8 slices Swiss cheese
1 can cream of chicken soup                                    
          1/4 cup white wine
1 c. herb dressing                                	                        
      1/2 stick butter
recipes.directions:
Combine soup and wine.  Mix.
Place chicken in 9x13 dish.  Top with slices of cheese.
Pour sauce over above.
Melt butter, mix with stuffing.  Spread lightly on top.
Bake 350 degrees 45-55 minutes.
recipes.notes: This is a very forgiving recipe (can adjust to your liking).  I used 2 cups of stuffing mix with 1 stick of butter to have more stuffing on top.  After about 40 minutes the top was getting too brown so covered lightly with foil.

recipes.title: Garlic Butter Sauce
recipes.ingredients:
1/2 cup soft butter
1 tbsp snipped parsley
1 clove garlic, crushed
recipes.directions: Beat ½ cup soft butter until fluffy (or just mash with a fork). Stir in 1 Tbsp snipped parsley and 1 clove garlic, crushed.  Refrigerate.  Bring to room temperature before serving.
recipes.notes:
Modify by adding garlic salt or onion or …
Makes good garlic bread.

recipes.title: Herb Rubbed Sirloin Tip Roast
recipes.ingredients:
1 1/4 tablespoons paprika
1 tablespoon kosher salt
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
1/2 teaspoon onion powder
1/2 teaspoon ground cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon dried oregano
1/2 teaspoon dried thyme
2 tablespoons olive oil
1 (3 pound) sirloin tip roast
recipes.directions:
In a small bowl, mix the paprika, kosher salt, garlic powder, black pepper, onion powder, cayenne pepper, oregano, and thyme. Stir in the olive oil, and allow the mixture to sit about 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Place the roast on the prepared baking sheet, and cover on all sides with the spice mixture.
Roast 1 hour in the preheated oven, or to a minimum internal temperature of 145 degrees F (63 degrees C). Let sit 15 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Pork Tenderloin with Honey Dijon Marinade
recipes.ingredients:
1 pork tenderloin
1 Tbsp honey
1 Tbsp soy sauce
1 Tbsp Dijon mustard
1 Tsp garlic minced
recipes.directions:
Mix together. Baste pork tenderloin for 30 minutes. 
Preheat oven to 400 f
Bake for 25-30 min (145f)
Let rest 5-10 min before serving
recipes.notes: If grilling, continue to baste as you turn the pork.  If baking, pour the remaining marinade on the tenderloin and bake. Double or triple the recipe as needed.

recipes.title: Hot ‘N Spicy Fondue Red Sauce
recipes.ingredients:
12 oz bottle chili sauce
1 medium onion, chopped (about ¾ cup)
¼ cup Lemon juice
1/8 cup salad oil
1 Tbsp apple vinegar
1 ½ clove garlic, crushed
1 Tbsp brown sugar
¾  tsp Tabasco
½ tsp dry mustard
¼ tsp salt
recipes.directions: Mix all ingredients in small saucepan.  Heat to boiling; reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes.  Serve warm or cool. Makes about 2 cups.

recipes.title: Macaroni and Cheese
recipes.ingredients:
3 cup dry macaroni
1 tsp dried onion flakes
Prepare according to the macaroni package.  Drain.
¼ cup butter
1 tsp salt
2 ½ cups milk
¼ cup flour
¼ tsp pepper
8 ounces sharp or extra sharp cheddar grated
recipes.directions:
In a heavy bottom pan, over medium heat, melt butter.  
Add flour, salt and pepper. Cook until bubbly. 
 Add milk. Stir occasionally until thickening begins, then stir constantly.  
When thickened to the desired consistency, add ½ pound grated cheese.  Stir until melted.  
Add macaroni and mix well.  Pour into a 2 quart greased casserole dish. 
Sprinkle with a few parsley flakes and dash paprika.
  Bake until edges start to brown slightly and are bubbly.
recipes.notes: Time varies with temperature. Can hurry the process at 375 degrees for 30 minutes or slow it at 325 degrees for about an hour.

recipes.title: Meatloaf
recipes.ingredients:
1 ½ lb lean (at least 80%) ground beef
1 cup milk
1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
½ teaspoon salt
½ teaspoon ground mustard
¼ teaspoon pepper
1 egg
¾ cups quick oats (can use regular oats)
1 small onion, chopped (1/4 cup)
½ cup ketchup with 1 tbsp brown sugar
recipes.directions:
Heat oven to 350°F. In large bowl, mix all ingredients except ketchup. Spread mixture in ungreased 9x5-inch loaf pan, or shape into 9x5-inch loaf in ungreased 13x9-inch pan. Spread ketchup over top.
Insert meat thermometer so tip is in center of loaf. Bake uncovered 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes or until thermometer reads at least 160°F. Drain meatloaf.
Let stand 5 minutes; remove from pan.

recipes.title: Pasta Salad
recipes.ingredients:
1 (12 oz) box tri-color rotini pasta
1 Bottle Italian dressing (Olive Garden) (start with ¼ to ½ cup and see how you like it)
1/2  to 1 cup finely shredded parmesan cheese (or other cheese; shredded or cubed)
Your choice of vegetables.  The amounts are up to you as are the vegetables.  I usually use broccoli, carrots, celery, onion and red or yellow pepper.
1 pint grape tomatoes, halved
1 1/2 cups diced English cucumber
1 1/2 cups tiny chopped broccoli florets
1 cup chopped yellow bell pepper
1 cup olives, sliced
3/4 cup thinly sliced baby carrots
1/3 cup chopped red onion
Your choice of meats or none
Cubed ham
Pepperoni
Salami
recipes.directions:
Chop up all the vegetables and meats to your liking.
Place the harder ones (broccoli, carrots, cauliflower) in the strainer.
Cook pasta according to package instructions to al dente but do not season water with salt (the dressing has plenty). Drain over the veges in the calander.  Let sit for 15 secs or so and then rinse with cold water.
Pour pasta into a bowl, add remaining vegetables, meats, etc.
Pour dressing over and toss to evenly coat. Cover and chill at least 30 minutes (or up to 1 day). Add a little more dressing if needed.

recipes.title: Penne alla Vodka
recipes.ingredients:
3 tbsp. butter
1 shallot, minced
2 cloves garlic, minced
1/2 c. tomato paste
1/2 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
2 tbsp. vodka
Kosher salt
1 lb. tubed pasta, such as penne or rigatoni
1/2 c. heavy cream
1/2 c. freshly grated Parmesan, plus more for serving
Basil, for serving
recipes.directions:
In a large skillet over medium heat, melt butter. Add shallot and garlic and cook, stirring frequently, until softened, 4 to 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring frequently, until paste has coated shallots and garlic and is beginning to darken, 5 minutes.
Add vodka to pot and stir to incorporate, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Turn off heat.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook pasta until al dente. Reserve 2 cups of pasta water before draining.
Return sauce to medium heat and add 1/4 cup of pasta water and heavy cream, stirring to combine. Add half the Parmesan and stir until melted. Turn off heat and stir in cooked pasta. Fold in remaining Parmesan, adding more pasta water (about a tablespoon at a time) if the sauce is looking dry. Season with salt if needed. Serve topped with more Parmesan and torn basil leaves.

recipes.title: Pierogi (Perohi)
recipes.ingredients:
3 cups flour
½ tsp salt
2 eggs
Scant cup of water
Potato Cheese Filling:
6 to 8 potatoes pared and quartered
¼  lb. Longhorn or Cheddar cheese, shredded
Salt to taste
recipes.directions:
Mix flour and salt on pastry board or in deep bowl.  Add the eggs and enough cold water to make a pliable, but somewhat stiff dough. Cover with cloth until ready to use.
Roll out dough to ⅛ inch thickness and cut into 3 or 4 inch squares or circles.  Drop about 1 tablespoon of filling into center of each circle or square (can make filling into balls for easier handling) and fold in half, pinching dough well all around the edges to sal well (Dip fingers in water and run fingers along the edge to hold seal.).
Drop phohi into boiling salted water (at least 2 quarts).  When perohi come to the top decrease the heat to boil slowly for 8 to 10 minutes.  Stir gently during the process to make sure all perohi are cooked thoroughly.  Drain and cool with cold water so that the perohi will not stick together.
Melt one stick of butter in skillet.  Saute onion until golden brown, heat pierogi in butter sauce or pour butter sauce over them as soon as they are drained.  Serve with sa dish of sour cream if you wish.
Cook potatoes in salted water as you do for mashed potatoes,. Wen tender, mash the potatoes, add shredded cheese to the hot potatoes and mix well.  Add salt to taste and cool before putting the filing into the dough.
Sauerkraut Filling:
Prune Filling:
recipes.notes: From Grandma Shuty (LaVerne Shuty)

recipes.title: Pineapple Soy Marinade
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup pineapple juice
1/4 cup soy sauce
1/4 cup brown sugar
4 garlic cloves, chopped
1 Tbsp. ginger
3 eye round steaks (about 1 and 1/3 pound total)
1 Tbsp. cooking oil
recipes.directions:
Mix together pineapple juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic cloves and ginger. Put in a zip-top bag and add steaks.
Add seafood or steak and marinade for 15 minutes.  No more than 1 hour.

recipes.title: Poached Salmon
recipes.ingredients:
1 lb. fresh salmon fillets
1 Tablespoon vegetable oil
1 small onion, finely chopped
¼ cup celery, finely chopped
1 Tablespoon fresh lemon juice
Dash black pepper
½ cup hot water
½ cup white wine
2 sprigs parsley
recipes.directions:
In a large skillet, saute onion and celery in oil until tender.  
Place salmon fillets on top of vegetables.
  Add remaining ingredients.  
Cover and simmer for 8 -1 0 minutes or until fish flakes easily.

recipes.title: Polynesian Sirloin Steak Marinade
recipes.ingredients:
1 1/2 pounds boneless sirloin steak, 2 in. thick
        1/4 tsp black pepper
        1/4 cup light molasses
        1/3 cup orange juice
        2 tbsp reduced-sodium soy sauce
        1/2 tsp garlic powder
recipes.directions:
Season the steak with pepper.  
Combine molasses, juice, soy sauce and garlic.  
Pour over the steak.  Turn meat once to coat completely.  
Cover and let stand in glass pan in refrigerator, turning occasionally, for 2 hours (or at least 30 minutes).

recipes.title: Hard Boiled Eggs
recipes.ingredients: Eggs
recipes.directions:
In a pot, cover the eggs with water
Bring the water to a boil (high heat)
Cover with a lid, take off heat, let sit for 13 min in the hot water
After, give the eggs a cold bath in ice water for ~5 min
Move to fridge

recipes.title: Rita’s Spaghetti Sauce
recipes.ingredients:
1 Tbsp olive oil
1 diced medium onion
2 minced cloves garlic
1 Tbsp dried basil (optional)
1 large can crushed tomatoes
1 large can tomato sauce
1½ cups water
1 large can puree
1 (6 oz) can tomato paste
recipes.directions:
In a large pot, lightly brown minced garlic and onions in olive oil.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer two hours.
recipes.notes:
I usually double or triple the recipe.
4 Tbsp = ¼ cup

recipes.title: Chicken Meatballs
recipes.ingredients:
1 lb ground chicken

⅓ cup dry bread crumbs

¼ cup parmesan cheese

¼ cup milk

1 egg

2 tbsp fresh parsley, finely chopped

½ tsp salt

½ tsp Italian seasoning

1 clove garlic, minced

⅛ tsp onion powder

⅛ tsp black pepper
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 400°F.

Combine all meatball ingredients in a bowl; mix well without overmixing.

Roll into 1½‑inch balls.

Bake 15 minutes or until internal temp reaches 165°F.
recipes.notes: Goes well with Garlic Parmesan Orzo or Ditalini Pasta

recipes.title: Garlic Parmesan Orzo
recipes.ingredients:
2 cloves garlic, finely minced

2 cups chicken broth

¾ cup heavy cream

½ cup shredded parmesan

½ tsp salt

⅛ tsp black pepper

1½ cups dry orzo pasta
recipes.directions:
Add broth to pan and scrape to deglaze.

Bring to a low boil.

Add orzo, cream, parmesan, salt, and pepper. Stir. Place meatballs on top, cover, and simmer 10 minutes until orzo is al dente.

Garnish and serve.
recipes.notes: Increase orzo to 2 cups and broth to 3¾ cups to serve more.

recipes.title: Lemon‑Pepper Fettuccine
recipes.ingredients:
Kosher salt
12 oz fettuccine
1 tbsp unsalted butter
1 large shallot, minced
1¼ cups heavy cream
1 large egg yolk
1½ tsp finely grated lemon zest
⅓ cup grated pecorino cheese (or parmesan), plus more for garnish
Freshly ground pepper
Crusty bread (optional)
recipes.directions:
Cook pasta in salted water.
Melt butter in a skillet; cook shallot until lightly browned. Whisk cream, egg yolk, and lemon zest. Reduce heat; add cream mixture and cheese. Cook until slightly thickened. Season with salt and 2–3 tsp pepper.
Add pasta and toss, using reserved pasta water to loosen. Garnish with more cheese.

recipes.title: One Pot Sausage Pasta
recipes.ingredients:
1–1½ lbs smoked sausage
½ diced onion
1 tbsp minced garlic
4 cups chicken broth
½ cup heavy whipping cream
1 can diced tomatoes
12–16 oz pasta
2½ cups cheese (cheddar, pepper jack, parmesan, etc.)
Seasonings to taste
recipes.directions:
Sauté onions and sausage in oil; add garlic for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes, seasoning, broth, and cream.
Add pasta, cover, and simmer 20 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes. Add broth if needed.
Stir in cheese.

recipes.title: Pork Roast with Rosemary
recipes.ingredients:
4 lb boneless top loin pork roast
1½ tsp salt
½ tsp pepper
1 tsp dried rosemary, crushed
2 cloves garlic, crushed
Pork Wine Gravy (below)
recipes.directions:
Season roast with salt, pepper, rosemary, and garlic. Place fat‑side up on rack in roasting pan. Roast at 325°F until internal temp reaches 170°F (about 2½ hours).
Remove roast and rack; reserve ¾ cup drippings for gravy.
Pork Wine Gravy
Add drippings to saucepan.
Stir in 1½ tsp flour and ¼ tsp salt; cook until smooth and bubbly.
Add ¾ cup water and ¾ cup dry white wine. Bring to a boil; cook 1 minute.

recipes.title: Slow‑Cooker Italian Pork Loin
recipes.ingredients:
2 cans (15 oz) cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
2 thinly sliced yellow onions
1 red bell pepper, thinly sliced
1 tbsp Italian seasoning
2 tsp salt
2 tbsp vegetable oil
2½–3½ lb boneless pork loin roast, trimmed
1 can (14.5 oz) diced tomatoes, drained
1 cup shredded mozzarella
¼ cup shredded fresh basil
recipes.directions:
Spray slow cooker. Add beans, onions, pepper, Italian seasoning, and 1 tsp salt.
Brown pork in skillet 3–5 minutes per side; transfer to slow cooker. Pour tomatoes over top. Cook on Low 3–4 hours (145°F internal).
Remove pork; stir bean mixture and transfer to platter. Slice pork and place on top. Add mozzarella, cover with foil until melted. Top with basil.

recipes.title: Classic Shuty Bread (Oat or All‑Flour)
recipes.ingredients:
1 tsp salt
1 cup quick oats (or flour)
4 cups flour
4½ tsp yeast
1½ cups milk
¼ cup oil
2 tbsp sugar
recipes.directions:
Mix sugar and salt in a microwave‑safe bowl; heat 1 minute.
Dissolve yeast in warm milk. Add oil and sugar mixture. Add oats. Mix slowly with dough hook until dough forms and becomes slightly sticky.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled (about 1 hour).
Punch down, divide in half, shape into two loaves, and place in greased 9×5 pans. Let rise again until doubled (about 1 hour).
Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes. Remove from pans and cool
recipes.notes:
Bread Machine Method
Place liquids in the machine first (warm milk 1 minute in microwave), then add remaining ingredients. Run machine as directed.

recipes.title: Classic Dinner Rolls
recipes.ingredients:
¾ cup milk
¼ cup water
2 tbsp butter
2¼ cups all‑purpose flour
2¼ tsp yeast
2 tbsp sugar
½ tsp salt
recipes.directions:
Combine ½ cup flour, yeast, sugar, and salt in a bowl. Heat milk, water, and butter until very warm; add to flour mixture. Beat 2 minutes on medium. Add remaining flour gradually to form a soft dough. Knead 8–10 minutes (by hand or dough hook).
Cover and rest 10 minutes.
Divide into 12 pieces; shape into balls. Place in greased 8‑inch round pan. Cover and let rise until doubled (about 30 minutes).
Bake 20 minutes or until done. Remove from pan and brush with butter if desired.

recipes.title: Pumpkin Spice Cookies
recipes.ingredients:
2½ cups all‑purpose flour
2 tsp cornstarch
½ tsp cream of tartar
¾ tsp baking soda
¼ tsp salt
2 tsp cinnamon
½ tsp nutmeg
½ tsp ground cloves
¾ cup butter
½ cup packed brown sugar
½ cup granulated sugar
1 large egg
2 tsp vanilla
½ cup canned pumpkin
Cinnamon‑Sugar Coating
¼ cup sugar
1½ tsp cinnamon
recipes.directions:
Whisk together flour, cornstarch, cream of tartar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves.

Beat butter and sugars until light and fluffy.

Add egg, vanilla, and pumpkin; mix on low.

Add dry ingredients to form sticky dough.

Chill at least 2 hours or overnight. Preheat oven to 350°F.

Mix cinnamon and sugar for coating.

Roll dough into balls, coat in sugar, flatten slightly.

Bake 8–10 minutes until tops look just set.

recipes.title: Chai Cookie
recipes.ingredients:
1½ cups all‑purpose flour
½ tsp baking powder
¼ tsp baking soda
½ tsp ground ginger
1½ tsp ground cinnamon
⅛ tsp ground cloves
½ tsp ground cardamom
¼ tsp salt
¼ tsp ground allspice
⅛ tsp black pepper
½ cup butter
½ cup brown sugar
1 large egg
2 tsp vanilla extract

Chai Sugar  
⅓ cup granulated sugar
¼ tsp cinnamon
¼ tsp ginger
¼ tsp cardamom
⅛ tsp cloves
⅛ tsp allspice
⅛ tsp black pepper
recipes.directions:
Whisk together sugar, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, cloves, allspice, and black pepper for sugar coating; set aside.
Whisk together flour, baking soda, cinnamon, ginger, cloves, allspice, cardamom, black pepper, and salt; set aside.
Melt butter over medium‑high heat until melted; turn off heat and mix until butter foams, 3–5 minutes until light and fluffy.
Add sugar and vanilla; beat on medium until combined. Add egg and beat until no streaks remain.
Add flour mixture and mix until combined. Chill at least 1 hour or overnight.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line baking sheet with parchment.
Roll dough into balls and coat in chai sugar. Bake 8–10 minutes until golden brown.
Cool 5 minutes on sheet, then transfer to rack.

recipes.title: Creamy Tomato Basil Chicken Slow Cooker
recipes.ingredients:
14 oz diced tomatoes with Italian herbs
2 cups tomato pasta sauce
½ tsp minced garlic
1 tsp salt
½ tsp Italian seasoning
½ tsp dried basil
¼ tsp black pepper
½ cup chicken broth or heavy cream
2 tbsp cornstarch
4 boneless skinless chicken breasts
2 tbsp finely chopped fresh basil
recipes.directions:
Add tomatoes, pasta sauce, garlic, salt, Italian seasoning, basil, and pepper to slow cooker.
Whisk broth and cornstarch; add.
Cook chicken 3–5 hours until 165°F.
Stir in fresh basil and serve.

recipes.title: CrockPot Little Smokies
recipes.ingredients:
1 package little smokies (about 1 lb)
1 cup barbecue sauce
½ cup brown sugar
2 tbsp apple cider vinegar
1 tbsp Dijon mustard
Pinch red pepper flakes (optional)
recipes.directions:
Combine all ingredients in slow cooker; cook on low 3–4 hours. Adjust seasoning as desired.
Serve with toothpicks or ladle.
recipes.notes: Add 1 tsp cornstarch at the beginning for thicker sauce.

recipes.title: Ditalini Pasta (Olive Oil Parm)
recipes.ingredients:
1¼ cups ditalini pasta
2 tbsp butter
2 cloves minced garlic
2 cups chicken broth
½ cup heavy cream
2 tbsp parsley
¼ cup parmesan
Salt and pepper
recipes.directions:
Sauté olive oil and garlic; coat pasta.
Add broth; bring to boil.
Simmer 8–10 minutes until liquid reduces.
Add cream; remove from heat.
Season; add butter, parsley, and parmesan.
Serve.

recipes.title: Lemon Orzo Pasta
recipes.ingredients:
1 tbsp salted butter
1½ cups uncooked orzo
2 cloves minced garlic
2½ cups chicken broth
¼ tsp black pepper
¼ tsp salt
⅓ cup heavy cream
¼ cup grated parmesan
2 tbsp fresh parsley
Juice of 1 lemon
recipes.directions:
Melt butter; toast orzo and garlic 2–3 minutes.
Add broth, salt, and pepper.
Bring to boil; reduce heat, cover, and simmer 10–12 minutes, stirring often. Add broth if needed.
Stir in cream, parmesan, parsley, and lemon juice to taste.

recipes.title: Sheet Pan Perogies and Sausage
recipes.ingredients:
1 (16 oz) frozen cheese perogies
1 large onion
3 tbsp oil
½ tsp black pepper
4 smoked sausages
recipes.directions:
Preheat oven to 400°F; line baking sheet.
Toss perogies and onion with oil, salt, and pepper; spread on pan.
Slice sausages and scatter around pan.
Bake 25–30 minutes, turning perogies halfway, until puffed and onions are golden.
recipes.notes: great with sour cream

recipes.title: Chicken and Dumplings
recipes.ingredients:
Soup Ingredients
2 tbsp vegetable oil
2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1‑inch pieces
2 tbsp unsalted butter
1 cup diced yellow onions
1 cup diced carrots
1 cup diced celery
6 tbsp all‑purpose flour
6 cups chicken broth
1 cup heavy cream
