½ tsp dried thyme
2 bay leaves
2 cups frozen peas
4 tbsp fresh parsley, minced
Dumpling Ingredients
1 tbsp butter (or butter with canola)
½ cup milk
1 cup all‑purpose flour
2 tsp baking powder
½ tsp sugar
½ tsp salt
recipes.directions:
Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium‑high. Brown chicken on both sides; remove and set aside.
Add carrot and onion; cook about 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium‑low. Add butter and flour; stir constantly for 2 minutes.
Add broth, cream, thyme, and bay leaves; stir well.
When simmering, add peas. Cover and cook 15 minutes.
For dumplings: mix flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt. Cut in butter. Stir in milk to form soft dough.
Drop spoonfuls of dough into boiling stew. Add parsley. Cover and simmer 15 minutes without lifting lid.

recipes.title: Chicken Noodle Soup
recipes.ingredients:
1 small onion, chopped (½ cup)
Chicken bouillon and/or vegetable bouillon
2 cups uncooked noodles
Chopped fresh parsley
1 5‑lb hen or stewing chicken
3 quarts cold water
2 medium carrots, sliced (1 cup)
2 celery stalks, sliced (1 cup)
recipes.directions:
Place chicken and cold water in a large pot; cover and bring to a boil.
Add celery leaves, inner stalks, celery salt, onion powder, and parsley flakes (optional substitutions allowed).
Cover and simmer 1 hour, removing scum as it forms.
Remove chicken and celery stalks. Skim broth and transfer to another pot. Add bouillon to taste.
Add 5 cups broth (if needed), carrots, celery, and onion.
Simmer 10 minutes until carrots are tender. Add noodles; cook until tender.
Add chicken back in and bring to a boil.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley.

recipes.title: Super Easy Chicken and Rice Soup
recipes.ingredients:
6 cups chicken broth + 1 cup water
½ tsp smoked paprika
2 cups cooked rice
2 cups chopped rotisserie chicken
2 tbsp chopped fresh parsley
1 tbsp butter
2 medium carrots, sliced (1 cup)
2 celery stalks, sliced (1 cup)
1 small onion, chopped (½ cup)
1 tsp minced garlic
recipes.directions:
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, celery, carrots, and garlic; cook 3–5 minutes until onions soften.
Add broth, water, and paprika; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 10 minutes or until carrots are tender.
Stir in rice, chicken, and parsley; heat through.

recipes.title: Navy Bean Ham Soup
recipes.ingredients:
1 lb navy beans (or other dried beans)
Small piece of ham or ham bone + 2 cups chopped ham
4 cups boiling water
3 whole peppercorns
3 whole cloves
1 diced carrot
3 ribs celery with leaves, chopped
½ sliced onion
1 minced clove garlic
½ cup mashed potatoes
Chopped chives or parsley
recipes.directions:
Soak beans according to package.
Add beans (or ham piece), peppercorns, and cloves to boiling water. Cook 2–2½ hours until beans are soft.
Add carrots, celery, onion, and garlic. If no mashed potatoes, add diced potatoes now. Cook 30 minutes.
Remove ham bone. Add mashed potatoes if not added earlier. Blend soup; thin with boiling water or milk as needed.
Add chopped ham and heat through.

recipes.title: Corn Chowder
recipes.ingredients:
6 slices bacon, crumbled
1 large onion, chopped
4 medium russet potatoes, cubed
4 cups chicken stock
2 tbsp all‑purpose flour
2 cups whole milk
1 can cream of corn (or frozen corn)
1½ cups fresh corn
½ cup sharp cheddar
Kosher salt
Black pepper
Chopped chives
recipes.directions:
Cook bacon in a large pot; remove and drain. Leave 1 tbsp fat in pot. Cook onions until translucent.
Add potatoes and stock; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until potatoes are tender.
Whisk flour into milk; add to pot.
Add corn, cream of corn, and cheese; cook 2–3 minutes until cheese melts.
Season with salt and pepper. Garnish with chives.

recipes.title: Horseradish Sauce
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup dairy sour cream
2 tbsp horseradish
1/2 tsp lemon juice
1/4 tsp Worcestershire sauce
1/8 tsp salt
1/8 tsp pepper
recipes.directions: Mix all ingredients together thoroughly; refrigerate until serving time
recipes.notes: Could also be used with beef or ham sandwiches

recipes.title: Bread Stuffing
recipes.ingredients:
¾ cup butter
2 large celery stalks, chopped (1½ cups)
1 medium onion, chopped (¾ cup)
9 cups soft bread cubes (about 15 slices)
1½ tsp chopped fresh thyme or ½ tsp dried thyme
1½ tsp salt
½ tsp ground sage
¼ tsp pepper
3 cups finely chopped apples
¾ cup raisins
½ lb bacon, crumbled (or bacon bits)
recipes.directions:
Melt butter in a 4‑quart Dutch oven over medium‑high heat. Cook celery and onion 6–8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender. Remove from heat.
Gently toss celery mixture with remaining ingredients until bread cubes are evenly coated.
To stuff a turkey: use to stuff one 10–12 lb turkey.
To bake separately: grease a 3‑quart casserole or 13×9×2‑inch baking dish. Add stuffing, cover, and bake at 325°F for 30 minutes. Uncover and bake 15 minutes more.

recipes.title: Rice Tacos with Queso
recipes.ingredients:
#Taco Rice:
1 pound lean ground beef
1 onion, diced
Salt & pepper
4 cups chicken broth
1/4 cup taco sauce or tomato sauce
1 packet taco seasoning
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1/2 teaspoon oregano
1 1/2 cups uncooked white rice
#Queso Sauce:
8 ounces Velveeta Queso Blanco cheese
3-5 tablespoons half & half
recipes.directions:
#Taco Rice:
Add ground beef and onion to a large skillet then season liberally with salt and pepper. Cook over high heat until meat is cooked through. Do not drain. Reduce heat to medium.
Add chicken broth, taco sauce, taco seasoning, garlic powder and oregano to cooked beef, mix well then bring to a boil over high heat.
Stir in rice then return to a boil. Once boiling, reduce heat to low. Cover skillet then cook for 25 minutes or until rice is tender, stirring the bottom of the skillet with a fork occasionally during the first 15 minutes of cooking. Do not open the lid or stir the last 10 minutes of cooking.
When ready to serve, drizzle mixture with queso sauce. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro if desired.
#Queso Sauce:
Combine Velveeta and 3 tablespoons of half & half in a microwave-safe bowl then microwave for 2-3 minutes or until melted through, stirring every 30-45 seconds.
Add additional half & half until sauce reaches desired consistency.
recipes.notes:
We need the pan drippings from the ground beef for flavor and richness but if your beef produces an excessive amount of fat, drain some of it if you prefer.
I save the taco sauce packets from taco meal kits to use in this recipe but you can also buy it by the bottle. Canned tomato sauce works as well.
Feel free to use whichever queso sauce you like best.
